mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month momentum in retail sales signals resilient nominal demand and near-term revenue support for index constituents, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Latest prompt snapshot reports RSAFS at 469092 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month RSAFS momentum does not produce higher 20-day SPX returns than lower momentum states in walk-forward tests, or Rank IC is below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer prices accelerate while the policy rate is rising, expected discount rates increase faster than earnings expectations, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 12-month change crosses above 6% and FEDFUNDS 3-month change is positive
evidence_refs: fact:Latest prompt snapshot reports CPIAUCSL at 242.348 and FEDFUNDS at 0.54
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with elevated inflation
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of walk-forward cases, or if the joint CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS signal has no incremental predictive power over either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in unemployment signals weakening household income and rising recession risk, causing investors to reduce expected earnings and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE 3-month change crosses above +0.3 percentage points
evidence_refs: fact:Latest prompt snapshot reports UNRATE at 4.7 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day SPX returns following the trigger are not below unconditional 60-day returns with t-test p <= 0.05, or if the hit rate for negative returns is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A downturn in industrial production signals weakening real activity and pressure on operating leverage, reducing expected earnings growth and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Latest prompt snapshot reports INDPRO at 103.8589 as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Reject if triggered INDPRO contractions are followed by average 20-day SPX returns that are not lower than non-triggered periods, or if the sign of the return spread is positive in two consecutive 3-year walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: A break lower in housing starts signals tighter credit conditions and weakening durable-goods demand, leading investors to discount slower cyclical earnings and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses below its 12-month moving average after being above it in the prior month
evidence_refs: fact:Latest prompt snapshot reports HOUST at 1090 as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST downside crosses do not precede lower 60-day SPX returns than upside or neutral housing states, or if the negative-return hit rate is below 50% in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate lowers discount rates and eases financial conditions, increasing the present value of future earnings and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS 3-month change crosses below -0.25 percentage points
evidence_refs: fact:Latest prompt snapshot reports FEDFUNDS at 0.54 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if easing triggers are not followed by positive average 20-day SPX returns, or if triggered returns are not statistically above unconditional returns at p <= 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth combined with stable unemployment identifies income-supported expansion without labor-market stress, supporting consumption and earnings revisions and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change is positive and UNRATE 3-month change is less than or equal to 0
evidence_refs: fact:Latest prompt snapshot reports PAYEMS at 145303 and UNRATE at 4.7
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-UNRATE condition does not outperform unconditional 60-day SPX returns, or if interaction terms add no out-of-sample explanatory power versus separate single-series signals.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation acceleration paired with industrial production weakness signals stagflation pressure, forcing tighter real financial conditions while earnings momentum fades and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized change crosses above 4% while INDPRO 3-month change is below 0%
evidence_refs: fact:Latest prompt snapshot reports CPIAUCSL at 242.348 and INDPRO at 103.8589
failure_condition: Reject if triggered stagflation observations produce non-negative average 5-day SPX returns, or if the joint signal is weaker than both CPIAUCSL-only and INDPRO-only signals in walk-forward testing.
